etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "tree") + phonetic 可(可) (kě) -> branch; axe handle
etymology.interpretation: The character 柯(kē, "branch; axe handle") combines the semantic component 木(木) (mù, "tree"), which suggests something relating to wood, with the phonetic component 可(可) (kě) to provide the pronunciation. As a branch and an axe handle are both made from wood, the semantic component directly relates to the material and function.

examples.chinese: 他用一根木头做了一个斧柄(他用一根木頭做了一個斧柄)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng yī gēn mùtou zuòle yī gè fǔbǐng.
examples.english: He made an axe handle out of a piece of wood.

examples.chinese: 树的每个分支都很坚固(樹的每個分支都很堅固)。
examples.pinyin: Shù de měi gè fēnzhī dōu hěn jiāngù.
examples.english: Each branch of the tree is strong.

examples.chinese: 他轻轻地触碰了那棵树的枝条(他輕輕地觸碰了那棵樹的枝條)。
examples.pinyin: Tā qīngqīng de chùpèngle nà kē shù de zhītiáo.
examples.english: He gently touched the branches of the tree.
